2606.19539.title: Review of Machine Learning Models for Solar Energetic Particle Prediction
2606.19539.paper_summary: Solar energetic particle (SEP) events have attracted increasing attention due to their significant radiation hazards for aviation, spacecraft electronics, and human missions beyond Earth's magnetosphere. From a scientific perspective, SEP events are intriguing because they arise from a set of physical processes extending from the solar surface and corona through the heliosphere, offering insight into particle acceleration and transport mechanisms that are widely applicable across astrophysics. Therefore, advancing our ability to understand and predict SEP events is essential both for deepening our knowledge of such mechanisms and for safeguarding space technologies and exploration. Traditionally, researchers have modeled SEPs using physics-based simulations and empirical methods. More recently, machine learning (ML) has emerged as a new tool for understanding and predicting SEP events. The purpose of this manuscript is to review the currently available ML models for SEP prediction, identify the datasets used for training, compare their architectures, inputs, and outputs, and, based on these insights, outline good practices and recommendations for future research.
2606.19539.paper_summary_zh: 太陽能粒子（SEP）事件因其對航空、太空船電子設備以及超越地球磁層的人類任務所造成的重大輻射危害而受到越來越多的關注。<lb/>從科學的角度來看，SEP事件引人入勝，因為它們源自於一系列物理過程，這些過程從太陽表面和日冕延伸至日球層，提供了對粒子加速和運輸機制的深入了解，這些機制在天體物理學中具有廣泛的應用。因此，提升我們理解和預測SEP事件的能力對於深化我們對這些機制的認識以及保護太空技術和探索至關重要。<lb/>傳統上，研究人員使用基於物理的模擬和經驗方法來建模SEP。<lb/>最近，機器學習（ML）作為理解和預測SEP事件的新工具出現了。<lb/>本手稿的目的是回顧目前可用的SEP預測ML模型，識別用於訓練的數據集，比較它們的架構、輸入和輸出，並根據這些見解概述未來研究的良好實踐和建議。
